transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center. This is Shirley, how can I help you?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Um, hi. I'm Shane. I... I almost didn't call. I've been trying to get the nerve up for weeks.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm really glad you did, Shane. It takes a lot to make this call and I want you to know there's no pressure here. Take your time. What's going on?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I've been using meth on and off for about three years. It's been more on than off lately. I'm not sleeping. I'm losing weight. My friends have noticed.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for trusting me with that. Three years is a long time to carry this, and the fact that you reached out today — that matters. Shane, I want to make sure I understand what you're going through. Are you using every day at this point?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Most days. Sometimes I go two or three days without and then I crash hard.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. And when you crash — are you getting any sleep during those periods, or is it pretty rough?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Pretty rough. I sleep a lot but it's not good sleep.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's a really common pattern with stimulant use. Our clinical team will want to do a proper assessment, but what you're describing — the daily use, the crash cycles, the weight loss — those are things they'll be looking at carefully. Have you ever tried to stop or get treatment before?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: No. This is the first time I've told anyone outside of my close friends.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Then this is a really significant step. I want to honour that. Shane, are you in a safe place right now? Any immediate concerns — medical, housing, safety?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'm okay. I have an apartment. It's just — it's getting bad enough that I know I need to do something.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That self-awareness is important. Let me ask — are you thinking about this as something you want to address in the next few days, or is this more of an exploratory call right now?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I want to actually do it. I've been thinking about it for too long already.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good. Let's make that happen. Do you have insurance coverage right now?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, through work. I have BluePeak Health.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great. Do you have your card available with the member ID and group number?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yeah. Member ID is BP70987905, group number GRP-185469.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. BluePeak Health, BP70987905, group GRP-185469. Our team will verify your benefits today. Based on your coverage and what you've shared, they'll be able to tell you what residential or outpatient options look like. Is there anything you're worried about in terms of taking this step?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'm scared of what people will think. My job, my family.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That fear is completely valid. A lot of people share it. What I can tell you is that confidentiality is something we take very seriously — what happens here stays here. And the people who love you, Shane? Most of them will be relieved, not judgmental. Our counsellors can also help you think through how to have those conversations when you're ready.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That helps. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Of course. Here's what we'll do next: I'm going to send you a short intake form and a confirmation email right now. Our benefits team will call you within ninety minutes with the verification results. You'll have a clear picture of your options by end of business today. Does that work?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, that's good.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. I'll get that email out to you in the next five minutes. And Shane — you made the right call today. We'll take it from here.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you so much.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Perfect empathy and rapport. Agent never pushed, gave space, and addressed confidentiality concern organically. Urgency triage scored 3 (acceptable) — caller is motivated but no same-day urgency, correctly handled.
